learn.title: Tidal Stream Energy Power
learn.intro: Tidal stream energy harnesses predictable ocean currents to generate power, offering a reliable and sustainable energy source. With consistent tidal patterns, this technology can provide a steady supply of electricity, making it an attractive option for coastal regions.
learn.c1_text: Tidal streams flow in predictable patterns, allowing turbines to generate power with high efficiency. For example, the MeyGen project in Scotland uses tidal turbines to produce up to 1.2 MW of power per turbine.
learn.c2_text: Excess energy generated during peak tidal flow can be stored in batteries for use during low flow periods. This ensures a consistent power supply, even when tides are not strong.
learn.c3_text: India has a vast coastline with significant tidal energy potential. The government aims to install 1 GW of tidal energy capacity by 2030, with projects like the Gulf of Kutch pilot underway.
learn.stat1: India: 1 GW tidal energy target by 2030
learn.stat3: Global tidal energy market: $1.5 billion by 2025

learn.title: Lithium-Ion Battery Storage
learn.intro: Lithium-ion batteries store energy from renewable sources, providing a stable power supply. They are widely used in electric vehicles and grid storage, helping to balance energy demand and supply.
learn.c1_text: Lithium-ion batteries use lithium ions moving between electrodes to store energy. They offer high energy density, making them ideal for portable devices and EVs.
learn.c2_text: Grid-scale lithium-ion batteries store excess solar or wind energy, releasing it during peak demand. The Hornsdale Power Reserve in Australia provides 100 MW of power, reducing grid instability.
learn.c3_text: India plans to install 15 GW of battery storage by 2022, supporting its renewable energy targets. The government offers subsidies for battery storage systems under the National Energy Storage Mission.
learn.stat1: India: 15 GW battery storage target by 2022
learn.stat3: Global lithium-ion battery market: $50 billion by 2025
learn.stat4: India's EV sales: 1.5 million units in 2023

learn.title: Building Integrated Photovoltaics
learn.intro: BIPV integrates solar panels into building materials, enhancing aesthetics and efficiency. This technology reduces energy costs and carbon footprints while blending seamlessly with architecture.
learn.c1_text: BIPV uses solar cells in windows, roofs, and facades. For example, the Solar Roof by Tesla generates power while maintaining a modern look, with 22% efficiency.
learn.c2_text: BIPV systems can reduce building energy use by up to 30%. The Indian Institute of Technology in Delhi uses BIPV to meet 40% of its energy needs.
learn.c3_text: India's solar mission promotes BIPV for urban areas. The government provides incentives for BIPV installations, with projects like the IIT-Bombay campus showcasing its potential.

learn.title: Wind Turbine Lifecycle
learn.intro: Wind turbines have a lifespan of 20-25 years, requiring regular maintenance and end-of-life recycling. Proper management ensures sustainable energy production and reduces environmental impact.
learn.c1_text: Wind turbines typically operate for 20-25 years. Regular maintenance, such as blade inspections and gearbox checks, ensures optimal performance and longevity.
learn.c2_text: End-of-life blades are recycled into materials like cement or playground surfaces. The Global Wind Energy Council reports that 85% of turbine components are recyclable.
learn.c3_text: India's wind sector focuses on blade recycling. The National Institute of Wind Energy (NIWE) is developing guidelines for sustainable end-of-life management of turbine components.

learn.title: Small Wind Turbines
learn.intro: Small wind turbines generate power for homes and communities, offering energy independence. They are ideal for remote areas and reduce reliance on the grid.
learn.c1_text: Small turbines convert wind energy into electricity using a rotor and generator. A 10 kW turbine can power a home, generating 10,000 kWh annually in windy regions.
learn.c2_text: Communities use small turbines to meet local energy needs. The Indian village of Khandwa uses small turbines to power 50 homes, reducing diesel dependence.
learn.c3_text: India's small wind market is growing, with projects in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat. The government offers subsidies for small turbines under the Jawaharlal Nehru National Solar Mission.
learn.stat1: India: 50 homes powered by Khandwa turbines

learn.title: Vehicle-to-Grid Technology
learn.intro: V2G technology allows electric vehicles to return energy to the grid, stabilizing power supply. This innovation supports grid resilience and maximizes EV battery utility.
learn.c1_text: V2G systems enable EVs to discharge stored energy back to the grid during peak demand. For example, Nissan Leafs in the UK provide 10 kW of power for 15 minutes, stabilizing local grids.
learn.c2_text: V2G reduces the need for peaking power plants by balancing supply and demand. The US Department of Energy reports that 100,000 EVs could provide 200 MW of grid support.
learn.c3_text: India's EV policy promotes V2G. The government is testing V2G in cities like Delhi, aiming to integrate 1 million EVs into the grid by 2025.
learn.stat1: India: 1 million EVs for V2G by 2025
